page: OVHcloud r3-1024 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system is configured with a single thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r3-1024 demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU benchmarks, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Its memory architecture is highly efficient, registering top-tier results in small-block cached read bandwidth and PassMark memory scores. For database operations, the instance handles high-throughput workloads effectively, as evidenced by its top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and strong results in PassMark database operations.

While the server excels at parallel CPU tasks and memory-intensive operations, it exhibits limitations in workloads requiring hardware acceleration. Lacking a dedicated GPU, its performance in large-scale machine learning text generation is constrained, with text generation for a 70B parameter LLM falling into the bottom 10% of benchmarks. However, prompt processing speeds for small and medium models remain strong. This profile makes the r3-1024 highly suitable for large in-memory databases, high-throughput caching layers, software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: The OVHcloud r3-1024 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor, it features 128 dedicated physical cores running at 2.3 GHz with hyperthreading disabled, paired with 1024.0 GB of RAM and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations, supported by a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While it lacks GPU acceleration—resulting in lower performance for large-scale LLM text generation—its massive memory capacity and high multi-threaded processing capabilities make it highly efficient for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and complex data processing tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-1024 is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 1024 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud r3-1024 is a memory-optimized server with 128 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 1024 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and NVMe storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with hyperthreading disabled
1024 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
400 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and heavy data processing
